description_long: This is a double-edged battle axe with a robust, metallic head featuring intricate engravings and a dramatic, sweeping lower blade. The axe head is primarily dark steel with gold accents at the central mounting and along the blade's spine, giving it a decorative yet formidable appearance. The handle is long and cylindrical, wrapped in a textured, light-colored grip at the base and capped with gold fittings at both ends. The shaft appears to be made of a dark, possibly metallic or reinforced material, segmented by gold rings for added strength and visual contrast. The overall design is both functional and ornamental, suggesting it could serve as a ceremonial or display piece as well as a practical tool. The craftsmanship and materials indicate durability and a high level of detail, making it stand out in an urban or historical context.
description: A double-edged, ornate battle axe with a dark engraved blade, gold accents, and a long, wrapped handle. The design is both decorative and functional, with detailed craftsmanship.
description_view_0: Side view showing the full profile of the axe, highlighting the sweeping lower blade and gold-accented head.
description_view_1: Edge-on view displaying the thinness of the blade and the alignment of the handle, with minimal detail visible.
description_view_2: Opposite side view, mirroring view 0, with the blade's engravings and handle wrapping visible.
description_view_3: Rear view focusing on the end cap of the handle and the narrow profile of the blade.
attribute_handle_type: Long, straight cylindrical handle with a light-colored, textured grip and gold end caps; likely wood or composite core wrapped in leather.
attribute_blade_design: Double-edged, asymmetrical blade with a sweeping lower edge, pointed upper edge, and intricate engravings; gold accent at the mounting.
attribute_mounting_style: Traditional wedged mounting with visible gold collar and reinforced central attachment.
